jokes.text: Well, you are what you eat.
jokes.setup: Phil rhapsodizes about Thanksgiving foods including 'that big old Butterball' turkey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implies Phil is a big old Butterball himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - using highbrow delivery for a lowbrow fat joke. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Did you just call me a big old Butterball? Nothing gets past you, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil realizes Geoffrey just called him fat
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic confirmation with mock surprise
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's dry wit shines - the butler's insubordination disguised as servility is his signature move.

jokes.text: His corpulent flesh rolls around his bones like a thick chocolate pudding.
jokes.setup: Phil demands Geoffrey be more creative in his fat jokes
jokes.punchline: Phil demonstrates with overly elaborate self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Absurdly poetic language for a fat joke. Phil's ego makes him improve his own insults - pure character comedy.

jokes.text: He is so huge that food comes to him from the gravitational pull alone.
jokes.setup: Phil continues demonstrating 'scientific' fat jokes
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous physics-based insult about his own weight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turning obesity into cosmic phenomenon. The specificity and absurdity make it memorable.

jokes.text: Is that your head or is your neck blowing a bubble?
jokes.setup: Phil demonstrates 'quizzical' approach to fat jokes
jokes.punchline: Surreal visual metaphor for double chin
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist imagery - the visual is so specific and weird it becomes art. Phil writing his own roast is perfect character work.

jokes.text: That elephant that trampled all them people called, he want his butt back.
jokes.setup: Will enters and sees Phil discussing creative insults
jokes.punchline: Will delivers his own version - crude but effective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will - cuts through Phil's pretension with street-smart directness. The contrast is the joke.

jokes.text: Whatever the last bid was, you raise the paddle and double it.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains her love of auctions
jokes.punchline: Reveals completely backwards understanding of how bidding works
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Vivian's wealth makes her oblivious to normal auction strategy. Rich people problems.

jokes.text: You know, it could be a girl or a guy or some mixture.
jokes.setup: Carlton's beeper goes off and Phil asks if it's a girl
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awkward non-answer that makes it more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Carlton's nervous evasiveness creates suspense. The 'mixture' line is wonderfully awkward.

jokes.text: Why don't you just have him beep you?
jokes.setup: Will wants to meet Michael Jordan
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic callback to his mysterious beeper situation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Carlton's wit. The irony adds depth to his character.

jokes.text: Boy, Mike's really changed his looks since he started playing baseball.
jokes.setup: Family expects Michael Jordan to emerge from celebrity box
jokes.punchline: Susan Powter appears - Will's deadpan reaction to the misdirect
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery on a great visual gag. Will's unflappable reaction to the absurd makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Aw, please, man, getting rid of your beeper like getting yourself fixed.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks Will about getting rid of his beeper
jokes.punchline: Will compares beeper to male virility in crude but accurate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Great character insight - Will understands the beeper as status symbol. The neutering metaphor is spot-on for 90s culture.

jokes.text: Excuse me, but I'm, like, starting to sweat.
jokes.setup: Susan Powter has the family doing aerobics
jokes.punchline: Hilary stops exercising because of mild perspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - treats minor sweat like catastrophe. Her vanity trumps everything, even health.

jokes.text: I mean, you don't see me skateboarding with Montel.
jokes.setup: Hilary explains her hostility to Susan Powter as fellow talk show host
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to skateboarding with Montel Williams
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic. The specificity of Montel skateboarding makes it memorably weird. Classic Hilary non-sequitur.

jokes.text: Should've given Hilary the paddle. At least we'd be able to drive whatever she bought.
jokes.setup: Family stuck with Susan Powter instead of something useful from auction
jokes.punchline: Will notes Hilary would've bought something expensive but useless (a car)
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that shows Will thinking strategically. The car implication is a nice character touch.

jokes.text: I feel like I tried to snatch a cheeseburger from George Foreman.
jokes.setup: Will explains why he can't go out after Susan Powter workout
jokes.punchline: Specific boxing metaphor for how sore he is
jokes.explanation: Great cultural reference that works on multiple levels - boxing prowess and George's later grill fame. Specific and visual.

jokes.text: The Dana I was expecting would be sitting down on the toilet... not standing up in front of it.
jokes.setup: Will learns Lisa's friend Dana is male, not female
jokes.punchline: Will's crude but clear explanation of his gender assumption
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure. Will's directness about bathroom habits is character-appropriate crude humor.

jokes.text: Let things flow naturally, right? Where did you hear that crock of garbage?
jokes.setup: Will tells Lisa not to define their relationship
jokes.punchline: Lisa throws Will's own advice back at him when it doesn't suit him
jokes.explanation: Perfect reversal - Lisa uses Will's own philosophy against him. The timing and irony make it land hard.

jokes.text: I ain't gonna be sitting up in the crib like some Silly Putty sucker.
jokes.setup: Will decides to date other people if Lisa is
jokes.punchline: Creative metaphor comparing himself to malleable children's toy
jokes.explanation: Great Will vernacular - the Silly Putty metaphor is specific and visual. Shows his wit even when emotional.

jokes.text: Carlton, you know the chicktionary rules. Right. Three feet back.
jokes.setup: Will pulls out his 'chicktionary' phone book
jokes.punchline: Carlton knows the viewing distance rules from previous exposure
jokes.explanation: Establishes the 'chicktionary' running gag perfectly. The formal rules for such a juvenile thing is pure comedy.

jokes.text: You can, uh, think of tonight as an open house.
jokes.setup: Carlotta mentions Will being 'off the market'
jokes.punchline: Will uses real estate metaphor for dating availability
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that extends the market reference. Will's smoothness with wordplay is character-consistent.

jokes.text: I was just thinking about how they made Tom Cruise even whiter.
jokes.setup: Will suddenly pulls away from romantic moment, claiming movie thoughts
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse about Tom Cruise's vampire makeup
jokes.explanation: Good awkward cover-up joke. The racial observation is period-appropriate and shows Will thinking fast under pressure.

jokes.text: You see, schizoid is a character that I do. Wait till you get to meet love boy.
jokes.setup: Will tries to explain his romantic dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Creates fictional 'characters' to excuse his behavior
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe comedy - Will's desperate improvisation makes it worse. The 'love boy' promise is painfully awkward.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, we got the same barber.
jokes.setup: Phil mistakes Will for Susan Powter in the dark
jokes.punchline: Will jokes about their shared baldness
jokes.explanation: Quick, effective callback to Susan Powter's look. Will's ability to joke about his own hairline shows confidence.

jokes.text: And as you get older, it might even seem commonplace.
jokes.setup: Phil misunderstands Will's romantic problem as physical dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Phil's oversharing about his own sexual decline
jokes.explanation: Classic Phil overshare. The misunderstanding creates awkward comedy, and Phil's TMI is character-perfect.

jokes.text: That was a shortcut to the diner where she worked.
jokes.setup: Phil claims he used to jog when dating Vivian
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just to get to her workplace faster
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - transforms apparent fitness into romantic stalking. Phil's honesty about his laziness is endearing.

jokes.text: Phil singing 'My Girl' while Will starts crying
jokes.setup: Phil explains that sappy love songs started making sense when he was in love
jokes.punchline: Will's emotional reaction to Phil's demonstration proves the point
jokes.explanation: Beautiful visual comedy showing Will realizing he's in love

jokes.text: Don't be ridiculous, Susan. The whole family would love to go on a 10-mile jog.
jokes.setup: Phil hides pizza from Susan Powter
jokes.punchline: Phil's obvious lie while literally caught with contraband food
jokes.explanation: Classic caught-in-the-act comedy. Phil's absurd overcompensation while holding pizza is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Yeah, you filthy stinking enabler.
jokes.setup: Susan accuses Will of enabling Phil's eating
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top dramatic language for food enabling
jokes.explanation: Susan's intensity makes ordinary food issues seem criminal. The language escalation is absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: I ain't got no time for buns of brass and brains of bricks.
jokes.setup: Will dismisses Susan Powter's fitness lecture
jokes.punchline: Alliterative insult about her physical and mental qualities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - the alliteration and contrasting materials make it memorable. Classic Will verbal dexterity.

jokes.text: My uncle can't walk one mile in my uncle's shoes.
jokes.setup: Susan uses 'walk a mile in his shoes' cliché
jokes.punchline: Will's literal interpretation about Phil's physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of common phrase. The literal reading creates perfect fat joke while showing Will's wit.

jokes.text: Looks like Lorena Bobbitt got to this suit, huh?
jokes.setup: Will sees the fat suit Susan gives him
jokes.punchline: References then-current news story about castration
jokes.explanation: Topical reference that was edgy for family TV. Shows Will's cultural awareness and willingness to push boundaries.

jokes.text: Oh, that's how I check them for ripeness.
jokes.setup: Carlton catches Will singing love songs to a melon
jokes.punchline: Will's absurd cover story about fruit inspection
jokes.explanation: Good quick thinking under pressure. The absurdity of serenading fruit for ripeness is endearingly weird.

jokes.text: You ain't had no home training, that's what your problem is.
jokes.setup: Kids laugh at Will in the fat suit
jokes.punchline: Will's indignant response shows his hurt feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's vulnerability

jokes.text: but when she threw out the strawberry Pop-Tarts, she crossed the line.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains why he's abandoning healthy eating
jokes.punchline: Pop-Tarts are his dietary line in the sand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - treating junk food like a constitutional right. The specificity of strawberry Pop-Tarts makes it personal.

jokes.text: I'm retaining water.
jokes.setup: Will bumps into someone while wearing the fat suit
jokes.punchline: Uses medical excuse for his apparent obesity
jokes.explanation: Standard fat suit comedy elevated by Will's quick thinking. The medical excuse shows his smooth talking skills.

jokes.text: Well, it ain't Fat Albert.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if the fat guy following them is Will
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic confirmation with cultural reference
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that's self-deprecating. Will's ability to joke about his situation shows character growth.

jokes.text: And that's when he's gonna pounce on you like a cheetah on a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Will explains male friend manipulation tactics
jokes.punchline: Vivid predator metaphor for romantic pursuit
jokes.explanation: Effective nature metaphor that's both accurate and funny. Shows Will's street wisdom about dating games.

jokes.text: And that's when he's gonna pounce on you like a cheetah on a rabbit.
jokes.setup: Will explains Dana's friendship strategy
jokes.punchline: Vivid predator-prey metaphor for male behavior
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor that reveals Will's own tactics

jokes.text: Believe it? I've done it.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions if Will really believes his theory about male friends
jokes.punchline: Will admits he's used the same tactic himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation - Will's honesty about his own manipulation tactics. The brevity makes it hit harder.

jokes.text: Because I love you.
jokes.setup: Will explains why he couldn't be with another woman
jokes.punchline: His first declaration of love comes out mid-argument
jokes.explanation: Emotional payoff to the episode's arc. The accidental nature of the declaration makes it more believable and touching.

jokes.text: Tomato, tomato.
jokes.setup: Carlton corrects that Will said 'love'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey equates love and leather with pronunciation joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the classic 'tomato/tomahto' phrase. Geoffrey's cynicism about love vs. leather is character-perfect.

jokes.text: 'Leather'?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Will used the L-word
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's innocent misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Geoffrey's dry delivery

jokes.text: Carlton... I give you my chicktionary. May it be as good to you as it has been to me.
jokes.setup: Will ceremonially passes on his phone book to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Treats pickup directory like sacred inheritance
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that elevates the running gag. The ceremonial language for passing on a phone book is absurdly touching.

jokes.text: Carlton... I give you my chicktionary.
jokes.setup: Will performs formal ceremony giving Carlton his phone book
jokes.punchline: Treating little black book like sacred artifact
jokes.explanation: Good ceremonial parody showing Will's character growth

jokes.text: I can feel it. I can feel the power! Yes!
jokes.setup: Carlton receives the chicktionary
jokes.punchline: Treats phone book like magical artifact with power surge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton - taking the absurd premise completely seriously. His earnest reaction to inheriting a phone book is endearingly ridiculous.
